subject: PATIENT
heading: BRIEF HOSPITAL COURSE:__page_no__2
text: brief hospital course: [patient_name] is a 81 y.o. female with past medical history below who presents with chief complaint listed above 81-year-old female with altered mental state presented to the ed today, at 640 5 to 7 am. reportedly had a series of low-speed fender benders while she was driving the car today because of her altered mental state this morning. she did have a prior stroke according to the family and she is behaving similar to that. normally awake alert oriented x 4 and functionally independent. in the ed, nih 3, ct and cta of head completed - no intracranial hemorrhage on exam. neurology was consulted. nihss worsened on floor nih6-7 and she was unable to answer any question, right sided neglect and left gaze partial palsy. sympoms improved and mri brain was completed which showed no acute infarction just remote infarction and lots of white matter changes. eeg showed bilateral independent occasional-to-frequent (~10-20% of the record) intermittent slowing in the right > left fronto-temporal regions, indicative of focal neuronal dysfunction in these areas. additionally there is one example of a sharp wave discharge in the right fronto-temporal region, suggestive of an epileptogenic focus / cortical irritability in the area. no seizures are seen. with recommendation of prolong testing outpatient. she was started on keppra by neurology, neurology placed recommended continuing aspirin and not adding plavix given her fall risk. also, recommended to get lumbar puncture but patient did not want at this time, will need outpatient echo cardiogram which was ordered by neurology geriatrics was consulted recommending outpatient follow up with center for senior health acute, acute on chronic, unstable/uncontrolled chronic problems/discharge diagnoses: 1. acute confusional state with neglect - transient 2. no evidence of acute ischemic stroke on the ct head or mri brain a. cardio echo outpatient

text: brief hospital course: [patient_name] is a 81 y.o. female with past medical history below who presents with chief complaint listed above 81-year-old female with altered mental state presented to the ed today, at 640 5 to 7 am. reportedly had a series of low-speed fender benders while she was driving the car today because of her altered mental state this morning. she did have a prior stroke according to the family and she is behaving similar to that. normally awake alert oriented x 4 and functionally independent. in the ed, nih 3, ct and cta of head completed - no intracranial hemorrhage on exam. neurology was consulted. nihss worsened on floor nih6-7 and she was unable to answer any question, right sided neglect and left gaze partial palsy. sympoms improved and mri brain was completed which showed no acute infarction just remote infarction and lots of white matter changes. eeg showed bilateral independent occasional-to-frequent (~10-20% of the record) intermittent slowing in the right > left fronto-temporal regions, indicative of focal neuronal dysfunction in these areas. additionally there is one example of a sharp wave discharge in the right fronto-temporal region, suggestive of an epileptogenic focus / cortical irritability in the area. no seizures are seen. with recommendation of prolong testing outpatient. she was started on keppra by neurology, neurology placed recommended continuing aspirin and not adding plavix given her fall risk. also, recommended to get lumbar puncture but patient did not want at this time, will need outpatient echo cardiogram which was ordered by neurology geriatrics was consulted recommending outpatient follow up with center for senior health acute, acute on chronic, unstable/uncontrolled chronic problems/discharge diagnoses: 1. acute confusional state with neglect - transient 2. no evidence of acute ischemic stroke on the ct head or mri brain a. cardio echo outpatient

text: brief hospital course: [patient_name] is a 81 y.o. female with past medical history below who presents with chief complaint listed above 81-year-old female with altered mental state presented to the ed today, at 640 5 to 7 am. reportedly had a series of low-speed fender benders while she was driving the car today because of her altered mental state this morning. she did have a prior stroke according to the family and she is behaving similar to that. normally awake alert oriented x 4 and functionally independent. in the ed, nih 3, ct and cta of head completed - no intracranial hemorrhage on exam. neurology was consulted. nihss worsened on floor nih6-7 and she was unable to answer any question, right sided neglect and left gaze partial palsy. sympoms improved and mri brain was completed which showed no acute infarction just remote infarction and lots of white matter changes. eeg showed bilateral independent occasional-to-frequent (~10-20% of the record) intermittent slowing in the right > left fronto-temporal regions, indicative of focal neuronal dysfunction in these areas. additionally there is one example of a sharp wave discharge in the right fronto-temporal region, suggestive of an epileptogenic focus / cortical irritability in the area. no seizures are seen. with recommendation of prolong testing outpatient. she was started on keppra by neurology, neurology placed recommended continuing aspirin and not adding plavix given her fall risk. also, recommended to get lumbar puncture but patient did not want at this time, will need outpatient echo cardiogram which was ordered by neurology geriatrics was consulted recommending outpatient follow up with center for senior health acute, acute on chronic, unstable/uncontrolled chronic problems/discharge diagnoses: 1. acute confusional state with neglect - transient 2. no evidence of acute ischemic stroke on the ct head or mri brain a. cardio echo outpatient

entity: generalized enlargement of the ventricles and sulci
subject: PATIENT
heading: FINDINGS:__page_no__3
text: findings: ventricular system and extra-axial spaces: generalized enlargement of the ventricles and sulci is noted without extracerebral collection with mass effect. cerebral and cerebellar parenchyma: periventricular foci of increased signal intensity on t2 and flair are identified without mass effect or restricted diffusion, compatible with chronic microvascular ischemic change. remote thalamic lacunar infarcts are redemonstrated bilaterally. no additional focal parenchymal enhancing or nonenhancing lesion is identified throughout the cerebrum or cerebellum.

entity: transition of care inpatient transition to home (own)
subject: PATIENT
heading: History of Present Illness :: Interim History:__page_no__17
text: history of present illness interim history: transition of care inpatient transition to home (own) admission date [encounter_date] 30 day readmission no number of in patient admissions in last 12 months. 2 discharge date [encounter_date] discharge summary report received yes hospital course: [patient_name] is a 81 y.o. female with past medical history below who presents with chief complaint listed above 81-year-old female with altered mental state presented to the ed today, at 640 5 to 7 am. reportedly had a series of low-speed fender benders while she was driving the car today because of her altered mental state this morning. she did have a prior stroke according to the family and she is behaving similar to that. normally awake alert oriented x 4 and functionally independent. in the ed, nih 3, ct and cta of head completed no intracranial hemorrhage on exam. neurology was consulted. nihss worsened on floor nih6-7 and she was unable to answer any question, right sided neglect and left gaze partial palsy. sympoms improved and mri brain was completed which showed no acute infarction just remote infarction and lots of white matter changes. eeg showed bilateral independent occasional-to-frequent (~10-20% of the record) intermittent slowing in the right > left fronto-temporal regions, indicative of focal neuronal dysfunction in these areas. additionally there is one example of a sharp wave discharge in the right fronto-temporal region, suggestive of an epileptogenic focus / cortical irritability in the area. no seizures are seen. with recommendation of prolong testing outpatient. she was started on keppra by neurology, neurology placed recommended continuing aspirin and not adding plavix given her fall risk. also, recommended to get lumbar puncture but patient did not want at this time, will need outpatient echo cardiogram which was ordered by neurology geriatrics was consulted recommending outpatient follow up with center for senior health. date discharge summary was received: [encounter_date]

text: history of present illness interim history: transition of care inpatient transition to home (own) admission date [encounter_date] 30 day readmission no number of in patient admissions in last 12 months. 2 discharge date [encounter_date] discharge summary report received yes hospital course: [patient_name] is a 81 y.o. female with past medical history below who presents with chief complaint listed above 81-year-old female with altered mental state presented to the ed today, at 640 5 to 7 am. reportedly had a series of low-speed fender benders while she was driving the car today because of her altered mental state this morning. she did have a prior stroke according to the family and she is behaving similar to that. normally awake alert oriented x 4 and functionally independent. in the ed, nih 3, ct and cta of head completed no intracranial hemorrhage on exam. neurology was consulted. nihss worsened on floor nih6-7 and she was unable to answer any question, right sided neglect and left gaze partial palsy. sympoms improved and mri brain was completed which showed no acute infarction just remote infarction and lots of white matter changes. eeg showed bilateral independent occasional-to-frequent (~10-20% of the record) intermittent slowing in the right > left fronto-temporal regions, indicative of focal neuronal dysfunction in these areas. additionally there is one example of a sharp wave discharge in the right fronto-temporal region, suggestive of an epileptogenic focus / cortical irritability in the area. no seizures are seen. with recommendation of prolong testing outpatient. she was started on keppra by neurology, neurology placed recommended continuing aspirin and not adding plavix given her fall risk. also, recommended to get lumbar puncture but patient did not want at this time, will need outpatient echo cardiogram which was ordered by neurology geriatrics was consulted recommending outpatient follow up with center for senior health. date discharge summary was received: [encounter_date]
